title: Stable Diffusion: Aging Video Time Lapse - From 1 to 90 Years Old in 30 Seconds
description:
See Life Fly By: Stable Diffusion's Stunning 1-90 Year Time Lapse Video

🔍 Dive into the fascinating world of AI-generated aging! In this tutorial, I'll walk you through my process of creating a captivating time-lapse video that visualizes aging from 1 to 90 years using Stable Diffusion Automatic 1111.

🌟 Why Watch This Video?

- Learn innovative ways to use AI for creative projects.
- Enhance your skills in AI-based image generation and video editing.
- Discover tricks to improve the quality and consistency of AI-generated images.

👇 Subscribe for More:

Don’t miss out on more insightful tutorials on Stable Diffusion and AI creativity! Subscribe and stay tuned for future content.

#StableDiffusion #AITimeLapse #AgingVisualization #VideoEditing #AIart #TechTutorial #CreativeAI #AIimageGeneration
transcript: Ever wondered how we can visualize the process of aging?  I've taken this curiosity to the next level using stable diffusion automatic 11-11.  In this video, I'll show you how I harness the power of AI to generate a series of images  seamlessly capturing the essence of aging.  Get ready to see the transformation of age as you've never seen it before or maybe you did.  All through the innovative lens of automated image generation.  As I said, I am using automatic 11-11 installed on windows.  And for the checkpoint, I use the Realism Engine SDXL.  I've prepared a prompt in a way that lets me generate similar images.  I gave the character a random name so it can remain consistent.  I also specify an eye color, but that doesn't always work well with all colors.  I use a solid background to minimize flickering and add emphasis to the words front view for consistency.  For negatives, I have a long string of words that I sometimes use to get better photos.  The sampling method is DPM++ SDE Carus with 40 sampling steps and you can choose the image size.  I used a portrait video ratio in this case.  The CFG scale is set to 7. For the seed, I keep the same seed for all generations.  Now comes the automation part.  I use scripts and choose the XYZ plot.  For the X type, choose prompt SR where S stands for search and R for replace.  For X values, the first value you add is the text you want to replace.  In my case, I want to replace the value one as seen in one year old in the prompt.  Then after the comma, you add all the values you want to replace it with and I added numbers from 1 to 90.  Next, chat GPT for these numbers so I didn't have to write them myself.  Hit generate and then take a break as it will take some time to generate 90 images.  On an RTX 4090, it took 15 minutes. These are the results.  If some images look bad or the transition from one pose to another is too fast,  you can delete them and keep what makes the time lapse look good.  In cap cut for desktop, if you go to the menu and settings,  you can change the image duration from the edit tab by selecting a short value like 0.4.  Click save, then drag all your images onto the timeline.  Go between the first and second image and add a transition.  I use the mix transition, which you can add between these images using the plus sign.  Now select that transition and press the apply to all button on the right.  That's all. You can then hit the blue export button to create your video.  Let me show you another quick trick.  Using a similar prompt for a 30 year old woman with a white blouse and a different background.  I wanted to see how I could age her.  I have a similar image for a before and after comparison like you see in my thumbnail.  Since I use a fixed seed, I get the same image each time.  Now that we have the first image with the young woman,  we send it to image to image using this button.  Once it's there, we change the age from 30 to 70 and scroll down to use a random seed.  So we can try different generations until we get one that looks similar to our woman just older.  For DeNoise Strength, use 0.5.  As you can see, the result is very similar.  You can probably play around with control net and other features.  But I just thought to share this simple solution with just image to image.  You can check my YouTube channel for more stable diffusion videos.  Subscribe if you want to see more similar videos. Thank you.  Thanks for watching.
